{{Quote|That's no moon, Mr. Solo. It's a space station.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, in ''Star Wars 3''|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 3">[[Star Wars (1977) 3|''Star Wars'' (1977) 3]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 3: Death Star!''''' is the third issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Roy Thomas]], based on a story and screenplay by [[George Lucas]], illustrated by [[Howard Chaykin]], and first published on [[June 7]], [[1977]] by [[Marvel Comics]]. The issue features the story "'''Death Star!'''", which adapts scenes from the film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].

==Publisher's summary==
''The ''Millennium Falcon'' is captured by the Death Star, which has just destroyed another planet!''

==Plot summary==
[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] destroys the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] with the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], while the captive [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] looks on in despair. Meanwhile, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], with the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], are being transported to Alderaan in the ''Millennium Falcon'', [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. As they approach their destination they discover the remnants of the destroyed planet and are soon captured by the Death Star. While on the Death Star, they find that Princess Leia is being held there, and they attempt to [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue]] her from her captors.

==Development==
[[Tom Orzechowski]] is credited as "Orz" for this issue. [[Steve Leialoha]] is credited as himself as illustrator and as "Leia" in the colors credits.

The original cover price for this issue was 30 cents. There were also several reprint versions, easily identified by "Reprint" in the upper left corner of the cover or on the inside indicia, or by the price and number appearing inside a diamond on the cover with no date or UPC.

==Continuity==
In the film, the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] who leaves his post guarding the ''Millennium Falcon'' is designated [[TK-421/Legends|TK-421]]. In this comic, his designation is TX-421.

When the prison officer asks Luke Skywalker where he is taking Chewbacca, Luke responds, "Prisoner transfer from Block TX-138." In the film, Luke responds with, "Prisoner transfer from cell block 1138." Both are references to George Lucas's first film, [[Wikipedia:THX 1138|''THX 1138'']].
